heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 28 May 2026 when 225 were seen.

storm.status: The last storm was on 18 July 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 1st since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 21 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 03 July 2026.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: You are currently experiencing a heatwave that started on 2026-07-01 and so far it has lasted for 23 days.  This year there has been 3 heatwaves, the average number is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 28 June 2026.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 68.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (220°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 2903 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 6735 lux), cloud levels (up 748 m), and feels like temperature (up 1.7°C) when compared to today.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will generally be on the hot side. Outside; hot temperatures will prevail. Also, a strong muggy feeling will dominate and winds will be practically absent. Showers, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and bright sunshine will dominate the day. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
